raw_consumer_statement:
Citibank tried to steal money from me. 

I booked a hotel room in XXXX XXXX through Citi Travel .

In the booking confirmation, Citi fraudulently added an undisclosed cleaning charge, and an extra local tax.

1. Booking a hotel room, seeing a cleaning charge, I would certainly cancel. Citi decided to sneak in the charge, without disclosure. When I found out the addition of the numbers were wrong and confronted Citi, they could quickly explain it was a cleaning charge. Which means they knew about it all along, fraudulently sneaked in the charge in hope I missed it.

2. More egregiously, Citi decided to sneak in an extra local tax. Swiss regulations require hotel to collect the local tax directly from the guests. Which means I paid the local tax twice.

I believe I was not the only victim. Please help investigate Citibank for the truth and whole truth.

Citibank did apologize, without any signature, to me and offered to refund the cleaning charge in the form of ThankYou Points. Not a word about the extra local tax they sneaked in, despite I presented proof that I did pay which to the hotel directly. I thought the tax fraud from a bank would be more serious. 

Please give me justice. 

Best Regards, XXXX XXXX
